info.description: The alarming service is used to configure and manage alarms.
info.title: Alarming API specification

post.description: Creates an alarm in a new or existing event and immediately starts notifying users

403.description: no payment data or no access to requested resources

post.description: Returns a preview of a planned alarm using the given resources

get.description: Returns an alarm payload for requested alarm id

parameters.description: optional parameter to update alarm with data for querying user (e.g. replaced variables in message)
parameters.name: update_for_user

400.description: Passed id is in the wrong format

patch.description: Closes the alarm with the passed alarm id

400.description: Passed id is in the wrong format

parameters.description: should all or only own alarms be returned
parameters.name: type

parameters.description: limits alarm list output to passed amount.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: id of an event, to get all alarms for this event
parameters.name: event

400.description: Passed query parameters in wrong format

parameters.description: amount of entries in paginated list

parameters.name: offset

post.description: Creates an alarm template with the passed parameters

400.description: Passed template is in wrong format

Redeems an existing sharing key for the given organization.

parameters.description: id of the alarm resource template
parameters.name: id

400.description: Passed template is in wrong format

parameters.description: id of the alarm resource template
parameters.name: id

400.description: Passed id parameters in wrong format

parameters.name: templateID

parameters.description: id of the alarm resource template
parameters.name: templateID

parameters.description: id of the alarm resource template sharing key

API-TOKEN.description:
You can generate an API-Token in the permissions-settings of your organization.
All requests will be performed in the scope of this organization. Requests regarding users will fail.
API-TOKEN.name: API-TOKEN

Personal-Access-Token.description:
You can generate a Personal Access Token in your profile settings.
All requests will be performed as the user who created the token, with permissions as configured in each organization.
Personal-Access-Token.name: Personal-Access-Token

ActiveUsers.description: ActiveUsers contains a list of all userIDs currently active in a shift in the requested organization

Alarm.description: # This is used for the interaction with alarms
Alarm.title: Alarm describes an alarm payload

feedback.description: all feedback payloads for all notified users

organizationID.description: id of organization for this alarm/event

success.description: successful alarm; true, when all positions are fulfilled

AlarmCreationLabel.description: AlarmCreationLabel contains necessary data about a single alarmed label

AlarmCreationRequest.description: AlarmCreationRequest is used to create a new alarm with the given settings

alarmResourceTemplateID.description:
Contains the ID of a resource template which will replace the values of this alarm with the configured
values of the template

alarmTemplateID.description:
Alarm message template ID
given ID is used to replace alarm's message text while creating a new alarm
either this or Message have to be set

eventExternalID.description: ExternalID of the event defined by the user

eventName.description: Name of an event; if event name is passed, a new event will be created with the passed name

eventSeverityLevel.description: EventSeverityLevel is a number (from 0-9) indicating the severity of the event

message.description:
Alarm message text
either this or AlarmTemplateID have to be set

optionalContent.description:
Optional content when creating an alarm, significant keys are shown in the tables below,
keys that are not in the tables below have no effect on the alarm.

__location__
 key | description
---|---
 address | the address of the alarm in text
 coordinateFormat | specifies the coordinate system used for the location of this alarm. Valid values are: WGS84 (default), UTM, ETRS89, or GK
 latitude  | the latitude in the (default) WGS84 coordinate system
 longitude | the longitude in the (default) WGS84 coordinate system
 easting | the easting coordinate in the UTM, ETRS89, and GK coordinate systems
 northing | the northing coordinate in the UTM, ETRS89, and GK coordinate systems
 zoneNumber | the zone number in the UTM coordinate system
 zoneLetter | the zone letter in the UTM coordinate system

__storage api fileKeys__
 key | description
---|---
 fileKeyIDs | array of fileKeyIDs
 fileKeyNames | array of fileKeyNames

organizationID.description: ID of the organization to create the alarm in

scenarioID.description:
id of the alarmed scenario

DEPRECATED: this property is deprecated and will be removed soon, please use alarmResources instead

scheduledEndTime.description: Scheduled time for closing the Event

startTime.description: Start time for this alarm. Uses the current time if not set.

AlarmCreationResources.description: At least one of those arrays must contain data, otherwise the alarm can not be started.
AlarmCreationResources.title: AlarmCreationResources contains an alarm's alarmed scenarios, units, labels and / or users.

allUsers.description: alarms all users of the given organization and no other resources if set to true

labels.description: list of labels and their amount

AlarmFeedbackComment.description: AlarmFeedbackComment describes a feedback comment for an alarm

comment.description: Comment describes the text of the comment

defaultPosition.description:
DefaultPosition describes the position of the comment
If DefaultPosition is nil, it's not a default comment

feedback.description: Feedback describes the feedback of the comment

organizationID.description: id of organization for this comment

AlarmLabel.description: AlarmLabel contains data about a single alarmed label

AlarmList.description: # This is used for an alarm creation
AlarmList.title: AlarmList defines an alarm list payload

totalAlarms.description: amount of alarms inside the payload

AlarmOptions.description: AlarmOptions contains options that can be set for an alarm

possibleNegativeFeedbackComments.description: possible comments for a negative feedback, 100 characters maximum per comment

possiblePositiveFeedbackComments.description: possible comments for a positive feedback, 100 characters maximum per comment

AlarmPreview.description: AlarmPreview contains an example for a planned alarm with the given alarming resources

AlarmResourceTemplate.description: AlarmResourceTemplate contains all resources for an alarm resource template

eventSeverityLevel.description: EventSeverityLevel is the severity level for the given event

AlarmResourceTemplateLabel.description: AlarmResourceTemplateLabel defines a single label for an alarm resource template

AlarmResourceTemplateList.description: AlarmResourceTemplateList defines a paginated list of AlarmResourceTemplates

AlarmResourceTemplateSharingKey.title: AlarmResourceTemplateSharingKey is used to share AlarmResourceTemplates with other organizations.

AlarmResources.title: AlarmResources contains an alarm's alarmed scenarios, units, labels and / or users.

AlarmTemplate.description: AlarmTemplate defines an alarm template payload

AlarmTemplateList.description: AlarmTemplateList contains a paginated list of alarm templates

AlarmTemplateSharingKeyCreation.title: AlarmTemplateSharingKeyCreation is used to create AlarmResourceTemplateSharingKeys.

AlarmTemplateSharingKeyForfeiture.title: AlarmTemplateSharingKeyForfeiture is used to forfeit an AlarmResourceTemplateSharingKey.

AlarmTemplateSharingKeyRedemption.title: AlarmTemplateSharingKeyRedemption is used to redeem an AlarmResourceTemplateSharingKey.

AnyShiftActive.description: AnyShiftActive describes if any shift is active for the requested organization

EscalationModeConfiguration.title: EscalationModeConfiguration is used to configure all required parameters for an alarm in escalation-mode.

initialEscalationStage.description:
InitialEscalationStage references the first stage (and all previous stages) in our ShiftEscalation we use to
initially alarm the required resources.

shiftEscalationID.description: ShiftEscalationID contains the ID of a ShiftEscalation we use to alarm the required resources.

archived.description: defines if this event is archived

endDate.description: time where this event was closed

externalID.description: contains an external id for this event defined by the user

name.description: user given name for this event

scheduledEndtime.description: time this event is scheduled for close

EventAbort.description: EventAbort marks an event as aborted

EventCreationRequest.description: EventCreationRequest describes an event that should be created with the contained information

EventList.description: # This is used for the event list with pagination
EventList.title: EventList describes an eventlist payload

responseTime.description: time of feedback response if the user has passed location details

userDistance.description: distance to the organization location in kilometers

userDuration.description: duration to the organization location in minutes

FeedbackCommentDefaultReq.description: FeedbackCommentDefaultReq describes a request to change the default comments

FeedbackPercentage.description: FeedbackPercentage describes the percentage of an alarm feedback

FeedbackQuantity.description: FeedbackQuantity describes the quantities of alarm feedback

Geofence.title: Geofence describes a virtual fence built around a geographical location and a radius.

assignees.description: read-only list of currently active users in this geofence

latitude.description: latitude in degrees defining the location of the geofence

longitude.description: longitude in degrees defining the location of the geofence

organizationID.description: unique identifier of the organization that manages the geofence

radius.description: radius in meters around the location of the geofence

GeofenceList.title: GeofenceList is used to return a paginated list of Geofences.

Label.description: # This is used for the interaction with alarms
Label.title: Label describes a label payload

assignees.description: assigned user for this label, users who have paused this label will not be in this field

availableUsers.description: number of users currently available for this label

minimumUsers.description: amount of users that must be assigned to this label at minimum

pauseableUsers.description: users who have been assigned to this label and can pause or reactivate it. if this is nil, the label is not pauseable

LabelList.description: LabelList contains a paginated list of labels

LabelStrength.description: This payload connects labels with an amount of user for a strength.
LabelStrength.title: LabelStrength describes a strength payload

amount.description: amount of strength members for a specific alarm

OrganizationAlarmSettings.description: OrganizationAlarmSettings contains the configured alarm settings for an organization

OrganizationWithLabels.description: OrganizationWithLabels contains labels grouped by their organization

OrganizationsWithLabelList.description: OrganizationsWithLabelList contains multiple organizations with their labels

OverwrittenTagAlarmMessage.description: OverwrittenTagAlarmMessage contains an overwritten alarm text for a configured tag id

Position.description: Position represents a position in the alarm that is fulfilled by a user

ProtectedTagUsageRequest.description:
ProtectedTagUsageRequest is a request that an organization
can make for a scenario to use a protected tag

RankLabels.description: RankLabels contains all labels for the specific rank

labels.description: assigned label ids for this rank

RankNames.description: RankNames contains the organization specific names of the rank

Scenario.description: Scenario contains all values of a scenario payload

description.description: user given description for this scenario

instructionCatalogID.description: possible id of an instruction catalog

isLocked.description: IsLocked describes if the Scenario is locked for deletion or not

name.description: user given name for this scenario

sendPosition.description: send position to users after alarm is closed

units.description: all units by their ID in an array

ScenarioCreationRequest.description: ScenarioCreationRequest contains a creation request of a scenario

description.description: user given description for this scenario

instructionCatalogID.description: possible id of an instruction catalog

isLocked.description: IsLocked describes if the Scenario is locked for deletion or not

name.description: user given name for this scenario

sendPosition.description: send position to users after alarm is closed

units.description: all units by their ID in an array

ScenarioCreationTagConfiguration.description: ScenarioCreationTagConfiguration contains details about the tag configuration of a scenario while creating it

triggered_by.description: tags which will trigger this scenario

triggering.description: tags which this scenario will trigger

ScenarioList.description: ScenarioList contains a paginated list of scenarios and the total count of all scenarios available

ScenarioTagConfiguration.description: ScenarioTagConfiguration contains details about the tag configuration of a scenario

triggered_by.description: tags which will trigger this scenario

triggering.description: tags which this scenario will trigger

Severity.description: Severity represents a severity in an event

icon.description: Icon is an icon for the severity

level.description: Level is the level of the severity

name.description: Name is the name of the severity

organizationID.description: organizationID is the ID of the organization where the severity belongs to

Shift.description: Shift describes the intervals of a shift and the assigned users all user defined parameters

ShiftEscalation.title: A ShiftEscalation contains a set of Stages used to alarm users from an escalating pool in their specific order.

ShiftEscalationCreation.title: ShiftEscalationCreation is used to create a new ShiftEscalation.

secondsUntilNextStage.description:
SecondsUntilNextStage is the time in seconds between two ShiftSchedule stages.
Required for alarms in full-escalation mode.
If n = amount of given shift schedules, the amount of entries in SecondsUntilNextStage needs to be n-1.

ShiftEscalationHistory.title: ShiftEscalationHistory contains information about an alarm running in AlarmModeEscalation.

ShiftEscalationHistoryItem.title: ShiftEscalationHistoryItem contains information about a ShiftEscalation stage.

ShiftEscalationList.title: ShiftEscalationList contains a paginated subset of ShiftEscalation entries.

ShiftGroup.description: ShiftGroup contains a group of users assigned to a shift

ShiftSchedule.description: ShiftSchedule contains meta-information about the schedule and multiple shifts

SmartLabelConfiguration.description: SmartLabelConfiguration contains the configuration for smart labels

conjunctionType.description: in which way the labels should be combined

geofenceIDs.description: the list of geofences that should be combined

labelIDs.description: the list of labels that should be combined, at least 2 different ones

amount.description: amount of strength members for a specific alarm

labelStrengths.description: label with amount for this strength

StrengthConfigWrapper.description: This is used for the strength config and wraps all configurations inside an array.
StrengthConfigWrapper.title: StrengthConfigWrapper is the definition of a strength config payload

StrengthConfiguration.description: StrengthConfiguration contains the rank configuration for a specific organizaiton

StrengthMap.description: StrengthMap is used to keep track of each rank and it's assigned labels

Tag.description: Tag is used to annotate and execute certain scenarios across organizations

TagAlarmResourceTemplate.description: TagAlarmResourceTemplate associates an organization with an alarm resource template in the context of a tag

TagAlarmResourceTemplateSummary.description: TagAlarmResourceTemplateSummary holds the id and name of an alarm resource template referenced by a tag

TagHistoryEntry.description: TagHistoryEntry is a single triggered tag

TagHistoryList.description: TagHistoryList is used to return a paginated list of triggered tags

TagList.description: TagList is used to contain a certain amount of tags for pagination

TagScenario.description:
TagScenario is used to associate a full organization with a scenario
(and because we can not marshal a map[replication.Organization][]Scenario)

TagScenarios.description: TagScenarios is used to contain a list of scenarios triggered by or triggering a certain tag

TagTriggerRequest.description: TagTriggerRequest is used to trigger a tag in an organization with the given alarm resources

eventExternalID.description: ExternalID of the event defined by the user

eventSeverityLevel.description: EventSeverityLevel is a number (from 0-9) indicating the severity of the event

message.description:
Alarm message text
either this or AlarmTemplateID have to be set

optionalContent.description:
optional content (e.g. coordinates, fileKeys).  significant keys are shown in the tables below,
keys that are not in the tables below have no effect on the alarm.

__location__
 key | description
---|---
 address | the address of the alarm in text
 coordinateFormat | specifies the coordinate system used for the location of this alarm. Valid values are: WGS84 (default), UTM, ETRS89, or GK
 latitude  | the latitude in the (default) WGS84 coordinate system
 longitude | the longitude in the (default) WGS84 coordinate system
 easting | the easting coordinate in the UTM, ETRS89, and GK coordinate systems
 northing | the northing coordinate in the UTM, ETRS89, and GK coordinate systems
 zoneNumber | the zone number in the UTM coordinate system
 zoneLetter | the zone letter in the UTM coordinate system

__storage api fileKeys__
 key | description
---|---
 fileKeyIDs | array of fileKeyIDs
 fileKeyNames | array of fileKeyNames

Unit.description:
It can be alarmed directly or as part of a scenario.
Each unit has a set of optional configuration describing its current status and location.
Unit.title: A Unit is used to group an amount of users fulfilling specific labels.

externalID.description: an external, but per organization unique, identifier this Unit can be accessed with, nullable

UnitCategory.title: A UnitCategory can be assigned to a [Unit] and group multiple Units.
UnitCategory.example: drivable units and units on foot

UnitCategoryList.title: UnitCategoryList contains the paginated response of a list of all [UnitCategory]s.

UnitCreationRequest.title: A UnitCreationRequest is used to create a new Unit.

categoryID.description: id of the assigned unit category

externalID.description: an external, but per organization unique, identifier this Unit can be accessed with; empty or null to unset

isLocked.description: a locked unit cannot be edited

statusID.description:
the current status ID of this Unit as described by the organization's UnitStatuses; null to unset

Status Value | statusID
---|--
0-9 | 0-9
A | 10

unitParts.description: assigned unit parts for labels and their amounts

UnitList.title: UnitList contains a paginated list of units.

UnitPart.description: This representation is used internally when alarming a Unit.
UnitPart.title: A UnitPart describes which amount of people fulfilling a specific label are required in a Unit.

UnitPartCreationRequest.title: UnitPartCreationRequest is used to create a new UnitPart when creating a new Unit.

amount.description: required amount of users fulfilling the label when alarming this Unit

UnitPosition.title: UnitPosition contains the current position of a unit.

UnitPositionExternalIDRequest.title: UnitPositionExternalIDRequest is used to update the position of a Unit using its ExternalID.

UnitStatus.title: UnitStatus describes the status a unit could be in.

id.description:
identifiable status number

Status Value | statusID
---|--
0-9 | 0-9
A | 10

isAvailable.description: a Unit in an unavailable status will not be alarmed

UnitStatusExternalIDRequest.title: UnitStatusExternalIDRequest is used to update the status of a Unit using its ExternalID.

statusID.description:
the current status ID of this Unit as described by the organization's UnitStatuses; null to unset

Status Value | statusID
---|--
0-9 | 0-9
A | 10

UnitStatusList.title: UnitStatusList is a list of UnitStatuses for an organization.

unitStatuses.description: list of UnitStatus objects for an organization with unique IDs

UnitStatusSettings.title: UnitStatusSettings contains unit status settings for an organization.

alarmedUnitStatusID.description:
the status ID that will be set when a Unit is alarmed.

Status Value | statusID
---|--
0-9 | 0-9
A | 10

eventEndUnitStatusID.description:
the status ID that will be set on a Unit when an event ends.

Status Value | statusID
---|--
0-9 | 0-9
A | 10

organizationID.description: The ID of the organization to update.

UserRankAssignmentRequest.description: This is used for a user-rank assignment request.
UserRankAssignmentRequest.title: UserRankAssignmentRequest is the definition of an user to rank assignment request payload
